title: Auto Debt-Free Certificate Cost Calculator 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
description: Calculate the cost of a debt-free vehicle certificate (libre deuda) for selling your car in Argentina. Fees, requirements & processing times.
intro: An Auto Debt-Free Certificate (Certificado de Libre Deuda Automotor) is an official document issued by Argentine provincial or federal registries that certifies a vehicle has no outstanding traffic fines, taxes, or liens registered against it. It is mandatory for vehicle transfers, license plate renewals, and many financing or insurance processes. The base cost in Buenos Aires (Federal) is $15,000 ARS, though fees vary significantly by province and urgency level. Processing time ranges from 24 hours to 10 business days depending on the registry.
keyTakeaway: **Total Cost = Base Registry Fee + Optional Urgency Surcharge (20–50%) + Administrative Stamp Tax (0–3% of fee)**. In Buenos Aires Federal the standard fee is **$15,000 ARS** with a 48-hour turnaround; express service adds ~$7,500 ARS for same-day processing.
useCases: Transferring vehicle ownership to a buyer — the notary (escribano) requires a valid Libre Deuda certificate dated within 60 days of the deed signing.
Renewing annual vehicle registration (Patente) in provinces like Córdoba or Santa Fe, where registries block renewal until a Libre Deuda is presented.
Securing an auto loan or refinancing — banks and financial institutions (e.g., Banco Nación, Santander Argentina) require proof of no outstanding liens before approving credit against the vehicle.
Exporting or importing a vehicle through AFIP/Aduana Argentina, where customs clearance mandates a Libre Deuda certificate from the originating provincial registry.
explanation:
## How It Is Calculated

The cost of an Auto Debt-Free Certificate in Argentina is set by each provincial registry authority (Registro Seccional) and the Dirección Nacional del Registro de la Propiedad del Automotor (DNRPA). There is no single national fixed fee — each jurisdiction publishes its own fee schedule (arancel) annually.

```
Total Cost = Base Fee (Arancel Base)
           + Urgency Surcharge (if applicable)
           + Stamp Tax / Sellado Provincial (if applicable)
           + Gestoria Fee (if using a third-party agent, optional)

Example — Buenos Aires Federal (standard):
  Base Fee          = $15,000 ARS
  Urgency Surcharge = $0 (standard service)
  Stamp Tax         = $0 (exempt in Federal jurisdiction)
  Gestoria Fee      = $5,000–$12,000 ARS (optional, market rate 2025)
  ─────────────────────────────────────────
  Total (self-managed) = $15,000 ARS
  Total (with gestoria) ≈ $20,000–$27,000 ARS

Example — Córdoba Province (express):
  Base Fee          = $18,500 ARS
  Urgency Surcharge = $9,250 ARS (50% surcharge)
  Stamp Tax         = ~$555 ARS (3% of base fee)
  ─────────────────────────────────────────
  Total (express)   ≈ $28,305 ARS
```

## Reference Table

| Province / Jurisdiction | Standard Fee (ARS, 2025) | Express Fee (ARS) | Processing Time (Standard) | Processing Time (Express) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Buenos Aires (Federal) | $15,000 | $22,500 | 48 hours | Same day |
| Buenos Aires (Province) | $16,200 | $24,300 | 3 business days | 24 hours |
| Córdoba | $18,500 | $28,305 | 5 business days | 24 hours |
| Santa Fe | $14,800 | $22,200 | 3 business days | 24 hours |
| Mendoza | $13,500 | $20,250 | 5 business days | 48 hours |
| Tucumán | $12,000 | $18,000 | 7 business days | 48 hours |
| Neuquén | $17,000 | $25,500 | 5 business days | 24 hours |
| Salta | $11,500 | $17,250 | 10 business days | 48 hours |

> **Note:** Fees above are approximate 2025 reference values. Argentine registry fees are updated periodically by DNRPA resolution. Always confirm the current arancel at your local Registro Seccional before payment.

## Typical Cases

**Case 1 — Private vehicle sale in Buenos Aires Federal (self-managed):**
A seller needs a Libre Deuda to transfer a 2018 Toyota Corolla to a buyer. They visit the local Registro Seccional, pay the standard fee of $15,000 ARS in cash or debit, and receive the certificate digitally within 48 hours. Total out-of-pocket: $15,000 ARS. The certificate is valid for 60 days from issuance.

**Case 2 — Urgent sale in Córdoba with a gestoria:**
A seller in Córdoba has a buyer who needs the transfer completed within 24 hours. They hire a gestor vehicular who pays the $28,305 ARS express fee and charges $15,000 ARS for their service. Total cost to the seller: ~$43,305 ARS, but the certificate arrives the same business day.

**Case 3 — Bank loan in Santa Fe:**
A borrower applying for a used-car loan at Banco Macro (Santa Fe branch) needs a Libre Deuda dated within 30 days. They pay $14,800 ARS at the Registro Seccional, wait 3 business days, and submit the PDF to the bank's credit department. The bank accepts digital certificates with the registry's official QR code.

## Common Mistakes

1. **Confusing provincial and federal registries:** Buenos Aires city (Federal) and Buenos Aires Province have separate registries with different fees and processing times. Requesting from the wrong one delays the transfer.
2. **Ignoring the validity window:** Most Libre Deuda certificates expire in 60 days (some provinces: 30 days). Buyers sometimes pay for a certificate weeks before the sale date and find it expired at the notary's office.
3. **Assuming the fee is inflation-proof:** DNRPA updates aranceles multiple times per year due to Argentine inflation. A fee quoted in January may be 20–40% higher by August. Always verify the current rate on the day of payment.
4. **Skipping gestoria fees from the budget:** Many owners calculate only the registry fee but forget the gestoria's market rate ($5,000–$15,000 ARS in 2025), which can add 30–100% to the total out-of-pocket cost.
5. **Not checking for outstanding infractions first:** If the vehicle has unpaid traffic fines (multas de tránsito) recorded with the Municipalidad or the national SINIAV system, the registry will issue a certificate with observations or reject it outright. Fines must be paid before a clean certificate can be obtained, adding unexpected costs and delays.

No related calculators are currently linked for this topic. Check [Hacé Cuentas](/) for other financial and automotive tools as they are added.

faq.q: What exactly is an Auto Debt-Free Certificate (Libre Deuda Automotor) and who issues it?
faq.a: It is an official document issued by the Dirección Nacional del Registro de la Propiedad del Automotor (DNRPA) through its local Registros Seccionales across Argentina. It certifies that, on the date of issuance, the vehicle identified by its domain (license plate) has no outstanding liens, embargoes, inhibitions, or unpaid registered debts against it. It does not cover unregistered municipal fines unless those are cross-referenced in the national SINIAV database.

faq.q: How long is the Libre Deuda certificate valid after it is issued?
faq.a: Validity depends on the jurisdiction and the purpose. The standard validity is 60 calendar days from the date of issuance for most provinces, including Buenos Aires Federal. However, some banks and financial institutions require a certificate dated within 30 days of the loan application. The Buenos Aires Province registry also accepts 60-day certificates for transfers. Always check the specific requirement of the receiving entity (notary, bank, or registry) before requesting the document.

faq.q: Can I request the Libre Deuda certificate online, or do I need to go in person?
faq.a: Since 2021, DNRPA has progressively expanded digital services through its platform at dnrpa.gov.ar, allowing certificate requests online in many seccionales. Payment can be made via bank transfer or Mercado Pago. The resulting certificate is a digitally signed PDF with an official QR code for verification. However, not all Registros Seccionales have fully migrated to online processing — some rural or smaller registries still require in-person attendance or gestoria intermediaries.

faq.q: Why do fees differ so much between provinces?
faq.a: Argentine registry fees (aranceles) are set independently by each provincial registry authority following minimum guidelines from DNRPA. Additionally, provinces apply their own stamp taxes (sellos provinciales) ranging from 0% to 3% of the fee. Inflation also creates regional variation: provinces that update their aranceles more frequently (e.g., Córdoba, Buenos Aires Province) tend to have higher nominal fees at any given moment. The difference between the cheapest (Salta ~$11,500 ARS) and most expensive (Córdoba express ~$28,305 ARS) can exceed 100%.

faq.q: Are there any vehicles or situations exempt from the Libre Deuda fee?
faq.a: Yes, in limited cases. Vehicles transferred by inheritance (sucesión) may have reduced fees depending on the province. Official government vehicles transferred between public agencies are typically exempt. Vehicles classified as total loss (siniestro total) undergoing registry cancellation (baja de dominio) may also qualify for reduced or zero fees in certain provinces. Exemptions must be documented at the time of the request — they are not applied automatically.

faq.q: What happens if the certificate reveals outstanding debts or liens?
faq.a: The registry will issue the certificate with 'observations' (con observaciones) listing each recorded debt or lien (e.g., embargo judicial, prenda, multas SINIAV). The vehicle transfer or the specific process requiring the certificate cannot proceed until those debts are resolved. Judicial liens (embargos) require a court order to lift, which can take weeks or months and involves legal fees on top of registry costs. Traffic fines registered in SINIAV can typically be paid at the issuing municipality and the record updated within 5–10 business days.

faq.q: Does the Libre Deuda certificate cover unpaid municipal traffic fines?
faq.a: Only partially. The DNRPA system cross-references the national SINIAV (Sistema Nacional de Infracciones de Vialidad) database, which captures fines issued by national routes (Gendarmería, Policía Federal on highways) and municipalities that have integrated with the national system. However, many smaller municipalities have not yet integrated, meaning locally issued fines may not appear on the certificate. Buyers are advised to also request a municipal infraction report (informe de infracciones municipal) separately in addition to the Libre Deuda.

faq.q: How often do DNRPA fees change, and where can I verify the current arancel?
faq.a: DNRPA updates its arancel schedule multiple times per year — historically 3–6 updates annually — in response to Argentine inflation. The current official fee schedule is published at dnrpa.gov.ar under the 'Aranceles' section. Fees listed in this calculator reflect approximate 2025 reference values and may not match the exact fee on the day you request the certificate. Always confirm the current amount at your local Registro Seccional or on the official DNRPA website before making payment.

sources.name: Dirección Nacional del Registro de la Propiedad del Automotor (DNRPA) — Official Argentine Vehicle Registry
